description_long: This is a multi-tiered pagoda structure with four distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves that curve upward at the corners. The building features a traditional wooden frame with orange-brown beams and posts, contrasted by dark, tiled roofs on each level. The windows and doors are symmetrically arranged, with lattice patterns and sliding panels typical of East Asian architecture. A prominent staircase leads up to the main entrance, and balconies encircle each story, providing visual rhythm and depth. At the apex, a golden ornamental finial rises above the highest roof, adding a decorative and spiritual element. The overall design is balanced and harmonious, reflecting the elegance and craftsmanship of traditional Japanese pagodas.
description: A four-story Japanese pagoda with upward-curving, dark tiled roofs, orange-brown wooden framing, and a golden finial at the top. The structure features symmetrical balconies, lattice windows, and a prominent entrance staircase.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance staircase, symmetrical balconies, and the full height of the golden finial.
description_view_1: Angled side view revealing the left-side staircase, layered roofs, and the depth of the balconies.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back lattice windows and the tiered roof structure without the main entrance.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the right, highlighting the right-side staircase and the stacked roof tiers.
